title: Eventbrite OAuth2 tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don't need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Eventbrite OAuth2 access token detected in variable assignment. Eventbrite access tokens grant authenticated access to manage events, retrieve attendee lists, process orders, and access organization data on behalf of a user. Exposing this token allows attackers to read attendee personal information, manipulate events, and access financial transaction data. Revoke immediately at eventbrite.com/account-settings/apps.

Bearer token detected in authorization header near Eventbrite context. Bearer tokens in Authorization headers provide direct API access. When used with Eventbrite APIs, exposing these tokens enables unauthorized access to event management, attendee data, and ticket sales operations.
